_note: MOCK procedure result for PT-033. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: ERCP with plastic biliary stent exchange
procedure.indication: Scheduled (elective) exchange of indwelling plastic biliary stent for benign distal common bile duct stricture; asymptomatic, normal LFTs
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol) with anesthesiology present
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum; major papilla identified and cannulated; deep biliary cannulation achieved

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: ERCP with plastic biliary stent exchange. INDICATION: routine scheduled exchange of a 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic biliary stent placed 2026-04-16 for a benign distal CBD stricture; patient asymptomatic with normal LFTs. ANTIPLATELET MANAGEMENT: clopidogrel (single-agent for secondary stroke prevention after 2024 TIA) was CONTINUED without interruption — a simple plastic stent exchange without sphincterotomy is a low-bleeding-risk procedure; given the patient's stroke risk on monotherapy, no hold was indicated and this was confirmed with the endoscopist prior to the case. SEDATION: MAC/propofol. FINDINGS: The duodenoscope was advanced to the major papilla without difficulty. The previously placed plastic biliary stent was seen protruding from the papilla, patent and in good position; it was grasped with a snare and removed intact. There was good flow of clear bile after removal. Deep biliary cannulation was achieved over a guidewire without sphincterotomy. Occlusion cholangiogram demonstrated a short, smooth, benign-appearing distal CBD stricture, stable to modestly improved compared with the April 2026 study, with mild residual upstream ductal dilation and no filling defects/stones. Repeat brush cytology of the stricture was obtained. A new 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic biliary stent was placed across the stricture over the guidewire with brisk drainage of bile and good positioning. No sphincterotomy was performed. No immediate bleeding. IMPRESSION: Successful uncomplicated plastic biliary stent exchange for a stable benign distal CBD stricture. New 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic stent in good position. Repeat brush cytology pending. Clopidogrel continued throughout. Plan for next scheduled exchange in ~3 months and ongoing surveillance of the benign-appearing stricture.

findings.finding: Prior 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic biliary stent, patent and well-positioned
findings.location: distal common bile duct / major papilla

findings.finding: Short benign-appearing distal CBD stricture, stable to mildly improved vs 2026-04-16; mild upstream dilation, no stones/filling defects
findings.action_taken: repeat brush cytology obtained; new stent placed across stricture

findings.finding: New 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic biliary stent placed with good bile drainage
findings.action_taken: stent placement over guidewire, no sphincterotomy

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort after uneventful recovery from anesthesia. Advised to continue clopidogrel and amlodipine as before. Return precautions reviewed: fever/chills, jaundice, dark urine, worsening abdominal pain, or signs of post-ERCP pancreatitis.

followup.recommendation: Review repeat brush cytology of distal CBD stricture; call patient with result. Result must be reconciled against the benign April 2026 brushings — any atypia/malignancy escalates management
Schedule next routine plastic biliary stent exchange to maintain patency and reassess the benign stricture
Continue clopidogrel (single-agent, secondary stroke prevention) and amlodipine; no interruption was required for this low-bleeding-risk exchange
Monitor for post-ERCP pancreatitis and delayed cholangitis; patient to call for fever, jaundice, or abdominal pain

planted_followup_items.description: Repeat brush cytology of the distal CBD stricture is pending; result must be communicated and reconciled with the prior benign (April 2026) brushings
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with cytology result; if atypical/suspicious/malignant, escalate to EUS/repeat sampling and multidisciplinary review

planted_followup_items.description: Next routine scheduled plastic biliary stent exchange to maintain patency of the indwelling 10 Fr plastic stent and reassess the benign stricture
planted_followup_items.expected_action: book ERCP plastic stent exchange in ~3 months (approx 2026-10) so the stent does not occlude

planted_followup_items.description: Ongoing surveillance of the benign distal CBD stricture pending resolution or definitive management
planted_followup_items.expected_action: track stricture course across exchanges; reassess need for continued stenting vs definitive intervention

planted_followup_items.description: Clopidogrel was continued (not held) for this low-bleeding-risk exchange; confirm no hold instruction was erroneously issued and that the single-agent stroke-prevention indication (not DAPT for a coronary stent) is reflected
planted_followup_items.expected_action: verify clopidogrel uninterrupted; ensure chart indication corrected to TIA/secondary stroke prevention

planted_followup_items.description: Post-ERCP pancreatitis and delayed cholangitis watch after biliary instrumentation
planted_followup_items.expected_action: advise patient to report fever/chills, jaundice, dark urine, or worsening abdominal pain within 48-72h

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm burping up some air, but no fever and no pain in my belly.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and swallowed-air bloating after duodenoscope; explicitly denies fever and abdominal pain
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and gassiness are expected after an upper endoscopic/biliary procedure and settle within 24-48h. Reiterate return precautions for fever, jaundice, or worsening abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Benign self-limited post-ERCP symptoms with no pancreatitis/cholangitis features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will you have the results from the brushing you took, and should I keep taking my blood thinner like normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Question about pending brush cytology result and whether to continue clopidogrel; 'blood thinner' is the single-agent antiplatelet, which was continued and never held
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the record: brush cytology expected back ~2026-08-01, GI will call with the result and reconcile it against the prior benign April brushings. Confirm clopidogrel should be continued uninterrupted — it was not held for this low-bleeding-risk exchange.
contacts.rationale: Fully answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and reinforces the med-reconciliation guidance that clopidogrel continues.

contacts.verbatim: I had a bit of mild queasiness the evening after the procedure but it's gone now and I feel back to normal. Is that alright?
contacts.clinical_signal: Transient mild post-sedation/post-procedure nausea that has fully resolved; no ongoing pain, fever, or persistent vomiting to suggest post-ERCP pancreatitis
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; brief self-resolving nausea after anesthesia/biliary instrumentation is expected. Confirm she remains afebrile and pain-free, and restate that new or worsening abdominal pain, fever, or jaundice warrants an immediate call.
contacts.rationale: Resolved transient symptom without red-flag features; documents that the post-ERCP pancreatitis watch (F5) window passed uneventfully. No escalation.
